ActionItems: Lock in fixed-price material quotes before the fall framing jobs
Keep two active jobs running in parallel through the slow winter months
Revisit the shop rent at renewal and compare against a smaller yard
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Monthly break-even review for Meade & Sons Construction ahead of Brendan and Yumi's 15th-of-the-month invoice check-in.

CurrentState.Note: Revenue figures reflect active job billings; draws split between Brendan and Sean.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Two concurrent active jobs cover fixed overhead; a single-job month dips below break-even.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.
